mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative average forward return or a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals supply-side contraction that immediately pressures equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in greater than 50 percent of triggered months during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the CPI exceeding 0.4 percentage points triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of months where CPI inflation exceeds the 0.4 percentage point threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to produce a positive average forward return or yields a Rank IC below 0.015 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as markets price in sustained economic expansion.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 50 percent of quarters following positive GDP growth reports.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rebound in industrial production following a sharp contraction indicates supply chain normalization that removes earnings headwinds, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases for 2 consecutive months after a prior decline
failure_condition: The recovery signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of instances or shows no statistical significance in a walk-forward test.
